authors: Amirdelfan K, Yu C, Doust MW, Gliner BE, Morgan DM, Kapural L, Vallejo R, Sitzman BT, Yearwood TL, Bundschu R, Yang T, Benyamin R, Burgher AH, Brooks ES, Powell AA, Subbaroyan J
title: Long-term quality of life improvement for chronic intractable back and leg pain patients using spinal cord stimulation: 12-month results from the SENZA-RCT
study_question: Does 10 kHz spinal cord stimulation improve quality of life outcomes compared to traditional SCS?
population_assessed: Patients with chronic back and leg pain randomized to 10 kHz or traditional SCS treatment groups.
outcome_measures: Oswestry Disability Index, Global Assessment of Functioning, Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index, SF-MPQ-2, GIC
inclusion_criteria: Chronic axial low-back and leg pain patients; successful trial with ≥40% pain relief on VAS.
answer_to_study_question: 10 kHz SCS provided superior long-term quality of life improvements including better disability scores, sleep quality, and reduced device reliance.
